evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: data quality practices, including tests, validations, and investigating data issues; robust modeling and testing standards
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour-focused: emphasis on testing, validation, data quality, and governance as core responsibilities.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Moderate: manufacturing company without explicit financial/regulatory language in JD.
evidence.collaboration_width: Principal Analytics Engineer; Analytics Engineers (team); business stakeholders
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: dbt in responsibilities, team of multiple analytics engineers, established modern data platform migration underway.
evidence.jd_authorship: Design, build, and maintain complex, scalable dbt models; refactoring complex transformations
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Hiring manager: precise technical methodology (dbt, complex multi-domain transformations, SQLMesh alternatives named).
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: business stakeholders; data models for real world business processes
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Internal_data: building platform for internal analytics consumption and stakeholder support.
evidence.autonomy_level: Help shape and refine shared approaches; Guide and support the growth of other Analytics Engineers
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic: defining team practices, mentoring peers, setting standards — not just executing on specs.
evidence.ai_role: Support the team in using AI powered IDEs, coding assistants, agents, and automation tools
evidence.ai_role_explanation: AI user: candidate expected to use AI coding tools/agents to accelerate development and improve team productivity.
evidence.testing_framing: data quality practices, including tests, validations, and investigating data issues
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Responsibility: testing/validation framed as owned accountability in core responsibilities section.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: data quality practices; robust modeling and testing standards
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate loss aversion: reliability and quality are concerns but not compliance/regulatory driven.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Accelerate the migration from legacy DOMO pipelines to the modern data platform
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Fix/scale: task is to migrate and scale from legacy to modern systems, not greenfield build.
evidence.loss_aversion: Data quality practices, including tests, validations, and investigating data issues — preventing bad data reaching stakeholders.
